articles.title: Assessing SFL Corporation (SFL) Valuation After Strong Q1 2026 Results And Dividend Increase
articles.summary: SFL Corporation's Q1 2026 results, including US$174.48 million in revenue and US$26.08 million in net income, coupled with a dividend increase and extended share repurchase plan, have drawn attention. Despite a strong stock performance (up 58.17% YTD), Simply Wall St's valuation narrative suggests the stock is 6.4% overvalued, with a fair value of $11.73 compared to the current $12.48. The company's significant charter backlog and liquidity position it for growth, but container exposure and substantial capex commitments present risks.

articles.title: SPGM vs. SCHE ETF Comparison: Costs, Returns, and Risks as of May 2026 - News and Statistics
articles.summary: This article compares two ETFs, SPGM and SCHE, focusing on their costs, returns, and risks as of May 2026. SPGM (SPDR Portfolio MSCI Global Stock Market ETF) is a global fund, while SCHE (Schwab Emerging Markets Equity ETF) focuses on emerging markets. The analysis details their expense ratios, dividend yields, assets under management, one-year performance, and portfolio compositions, highlighting SCHE's lower cost but SPGM's better recent performance and lower drawdown risk.

The smoking guns in Trump’s new financial disclosure
articles.summary: Donald Trump's recent financial disclosure reveals instances where he purchased stock in companies on the same day or shortly before publicly praising them, such as Thermo Fisher, Apple, Micron, and Dell. These transactions, many marked "UNSOLICITED," contradict claims that his investments are independently managed and highlight a pattern of potentially self-serving public endorsements. The report also notes his failure to meet the 45-day reporting deadline for many trades, resulting in a $200 fine.
articles.content: Donald Trump's recent financial disclosure reveals instances where he purchased stock in companies on the same day or shortly before publicly praising them, such as Thermo Fisher, Apple, Micron, and Dell. articles.title: Moderna Inc. stock (US60770K1034): Shares slip as post-COVID reset keeps volatility high
articles.summary: Moderna shares experienced a slip as the company navigates its transition from pandemic-driven COVID-19 vaccine sales to a broader portfolio of mRNA vaccines and therapeutics. This shift has resulted in continued stock volatility, with shares closing at $49.04, down 1.98%, reflecting investor uncertainty about the pace at which new products can offset declining COVID-19 revenues. The company's future stock performance will largely depend on its ability to convert its scientific pipeline and cash reserves into sustainable, recurring revenue streams beyond its initial pandemic success.
articles.content: Moderna shares experienced a slip as the company navigates its transition from pandemic-driven COVID-19 vaccine sales to a broader portfolio of mRNA vaccines and therapeutics. This shift has resulted in continued stock volatility, with shares closing at $49.04, down 1.98%, reflecting investor uncertainty about the pace at which new products can offset declining COVID-19 revenues. The company's future stock performance will largely depend on its ability to convert its scientific pipeline and cash reserves into sustainable, recurring revenue streams beyond its initial pandemic success.
